hypothesis: Self-supervised contrastive pretraining on a combined unlabeled SEM image corpus of additive-free and additive-containing cathode samples will learn domain-invariant morphological representations that, when fine-tuned with limited labeled target data, outperform the two-stage supervised transfer learning (pretraining on additive-free data and fine-tuning on additive-containing data) in classifying cathode composition and aging state, particularly for domain-shifted classes.
